coredata.dc:title: Nanostructured molybdenum/heteroatom-doped carbon dots nanohybrids for lubrication by direct carbonization route
coredata.dc:creator: Cai, Tao
Zhang, Yunxiao
Liu, Dan
Tong, Dingyi
Liu, Shenggao
coredata.dc:description: Inorganic-organic hybrid ternary molybdenum-doped carbon dots-metal nanohybrids(CDs-Mo) have been prepared by in situ carbonization of tri-functional glutamic acid, bis-functional dicationic liquid([BHDABCO][Ntf]) and ammonium molybdate, which were explored as lubricant additive in PEG base oil that reduced the friction coefficient of 62.90% and wear volume of 80.21%. The positively charged steel surface attracted CDs-Mo during friction thus leading to the fluorescence decrease of PEG with 1 wt% CDs-Mo, which aggregated and transformed into uneven carbon-based oil film that avoided the direct contact of frictional pairs.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon dots
Nanocomposites
Microstructure
Wear and friction

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: TEM image(a), size distribution(b), UV absorption spectrum(c) and PL emission spectra(d) of CDs-Mo.

Schematic of the four-ball tester configuration(a), coefficient of friction(CoF) as a function of testing time(b), three-dimensional images of wear scar lubricated with PEG(c) and PEG+1%CDs-Mo(d) and height distribution(e,f) corresponding to the red line in the c and d respectively.

PL intensity of PEG+1%CDs-Mo having different concentration of ferric chloride, The λex the is 380nm (a, b). Optical image of the selected area for Raman spectroscopy(c), Raman mapping of yellow box(d), of which the change of color represented the size of the area of D- and G-band, and Raman spectra of the specified areas in the(e, f).

head.ce:title: Nanostructured molybdenum/heteroatom-doped carbon dots nanohybrids for lubrication by direct carbonization route

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Ningbo Institute of Materials Technology and Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences. No. 1219, Zhongguan West Road, Ningbo City, Zhejiang Province 315201 PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Ningbo Institute of Materials Technology and Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Inorganic-organic hybrid ternary molybdenum-doped carbon dots-metal nanohybrids(CDs-Mo) have been prepared by in situ carbonization of tri-functional glutamic acid, bis-functional dicationic liquid([BHDABCO][Ntf]) and ammonium molybdate, which were explored as lubricant additive in PEG base oil that reduced the friction coefficient of 62.90% and wear volume of 80.21%. The positively charged steel surface attracted CDs-Mo during friction thus leading to the fluorescence decrease of PEG with 1 wt% CDs-Mo, which aggregated and transformed into uneven carbon-based oil film that avoided the direct contact of frictional pairs.

ce:section.ce:para: Carbon dots(CDs) are carbon nanomaterials with sizes below 10 nm and unique microstructure, which have been considered as ideal lubricant additives due to their low cost, little pollution to the environment and potential tribological properties [1–3]. It is generally accepted that the well-dispersed nanomaterials in fluids can behave their full potential as lubricant additives. Many efforts have constructed the carbon-based hybrids with unique core-shell structure using ionic liquids(ILs) to improve their dispersion stability and tribological properties, and imidazole-based ILs are most studied. However, the novel CDs hybrids with bis-functional dicationic ionic liquids as constitutional units are yet to be explored.

On the other side, as a member of the transition metal, molybdenum has also been widely used in the field of oxidative desulfurization, catalysis and lubrication, etc. For instance, molybdenum complexes can selectively oxidize alkenes, alcohols and sulfides in the presence of H2O2 [4]. It is proved that molybdenum-based catalysts promote the formation of intermediate aromatic hydrocarbon for growing CNTs in high yield [5]. Molybdenum-based compounds can produce MoS2 tribofilm in the presence of ZDDP thus contributing ultralow lubrication [6]. It is believed that the strategy to incorporate the transition metal(molybdenum) with ultra-small size carbon dots not only exhibit excellent tribological performance in the field of tribology but also may bring some expected and unexpected properties in others applied circumstances.

To realize the assumption, the 1,4-Diazabicyclo[2.2.2]octane was used as bridging agent to synthesize novel bis-functional dicationic ionic liquid([BHDABCO][Tf2N]), which was directly pyrolyzed with tri-functional glutamic acid and ammonium molybdate to prepare target ternary molybdenum-doped carbon dots-metal nanohybrids(CDs-Mo). Then the tribological properties and mechanism were investigated.

3-chloro-1-propanol(0.2 mol) and 1,4-diazabicyclo[2.2.2]octane(0.05 mol) were mixed together with 100 ml methanol under a nitrogen atmosphere at 70 °C for 72 h. White precipitate was obtained by filtration and dried at 60 °C under vacuum for 48 h([BHDABCO][Cl]). 4 mmol of lithium bis((trifluoromethyl)sulfonyl)amide and 2 mmol of [BHDABCO][Cl] were added into 20 ml water and then the mixture was stirred for several hours under room temperature, afterward, 20 ml ethyl acetate was mixed with water solution to extract the [BHDABCO][Tf2N] (Fig. S1).

CDs-Mo were prepared by in situ carbonization of glutamic acid, [BHDABCO][Tf2N] and ammonium molybdate at 200 °C for 20 min with a ratio of 40/8/1. The few remaining sediments were removed using centrifugation (10000 rpm, 30 min). The supernatant was dialyzed overnight using a dialysis bag with a molecular weight cut-off (MWCO) of 1000 (Fig. S2).

Tribological properties of CDs-Mo were characterized using a four-ball tester with GCr15 steel balls (diameter, 12.7 mm; roughness, <0.032 μm; hardness, 59-61HRC). Polyethylene glycol(PEG) with an average molecular weight of 190–210 g mol−1 was used as base oil. Each tribo-test was repeated three times, and the standard deviation of each friction test was provided to understand the repeatability of the results.
The molecular structure of the products, tribological testing and surface analysis were given in Supplementary information.

CDs-Mo are spherical nanoparticles with a narrow size distribution of 4.47 ± 1.13 nm (Fig. 1 a and 1b). A broad peak centered at ∼305 nm is observed in the UV-vis spectrum, which is attributed to n-π* of CO transition (Fig.1c). The λem peak of CDs-Mo shifts from 400 nm to 525 nm when λex moves from 320 nm to 460 nm, which indicates λem-dependent λex exists. When λex is 380 nm, λex has the highest response peak (Fig.1d).

In the FT-IR (Fig. S3), the strong peak at around 1723 cm−1 is assigned to carboxylic ester CO, Simultaneously, the peaks at 1062 and 1351 cm−1 are assigned to the stretching vibration of C-F and the asymmetric stretching vibration of the sulfone group in CDs-Mo, indicating the existence of [BHDABCO][Tf2N] in CDs-Mo [7].

In the C1s spectra of XPS (Fig. S4), the 284.8, 286.5, 288.5, 292.6 eV are associated with C–C, C-N/C-S/C-O, COO and C-F, respectively.[8] The N1s presents two peaks at 399.7 and 402.8 eV, which are corresponded to the amine and quaternary N structures. 168.6 and 169.8 eV in S2p spectra are related to SO and S-C/S-N. Mo-O is proved by the 232.8 eV peak in the Mo3d spectrum, the results above indicate the CDs-Mo have been synthesized as designed.[9,10] The chemical compositions of CDs-Mo are shown in Fig. S5 .

Fig. 2 shows the coefficient of friction (CoF) and wear scar diameter (WSD) lubricated with PEG and PEG + 1% CDs-Mo. For PEG, the CoF started from ∼ 0.10 and gradually increased to ∼ 0.15 at ∼ 1650 s, indicating the tribo-film destroyed and scuffing process happened. When the CDs-Mo were added, the mean CoF lubricated with PEG reduced from 0.124 ± 0.0081 (neat PEG) to 0.046 ± 0.0046, which indicated 62.90% improvement. The mean WSD lubricated with PEG and PEG + 1% CDs-Mo was 0.934 ± 0.0274 mm and 0.623 ± 0.0486 mm respectively, which indicated 33.29% improvement in WSD, i.e. reduction of wear volume(WV) by 80.21%.

The anti-wear results were illustrated clearly by the 3D images of wear scar (Fig.2c-f). The worn surface lubricated with CDs-Mo showed smaller and shallower worn area than that of PEG. The above results indicated that the as-prepared CDs-Mo as additives showed effective anti-wear and friction-reducing properties under severe frictional condition.

The well-dispersed CDs-Mo exhibited blue photoluminescence at ∼440 nm (λem) when the λ ex was 380 nm. We further characterized the PL spectrum of the tested PEG oil sample after tribo-test, impressively, the tested oil sample still exhibited PL nature. However, itself PL intensity had a decrease of ∼ 50% in comparison with that of initial oil sample (Fig. S6).

Energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) was used to characterize the chemical compositions on worn scars (Fig.S7). It can be concluded that the CDs-Mo transfer to the contact zone and form a lubricious tribo-film thus avoiding undesirable friction and wear [11].

As is generally accepted, the low energy electrons can be emitted during the rubbing process, and tiny convex spots with positive charged are formed on the surface. For steel-steel contact model, the escape of electrons refers to the multiple Fe ions with positive charge generated on the surface. It is therefore inferred the CDs-Mo have strong interactions with Fe ions. For simplicity, Fe(III) ions are selected as a representative ion on tiny convex spots. Based on previous theory, the hydroxyl and amine groups of CDs can interact with Fe(III) in a way thereby forming complex coordinated negative ions [12,13]. The emission intensities of PEG + 1% CDs-Mo dependence on concentrations of ferric chloride (1 mM, 2 mM, 3 mM, 6 mM) were investigated. As shown in Fig. 3 a and b, the response PL intensity decreased gradually when the concentration of Fe(III) was increased, which reduced by around 95% when the Fe(III) concentration reaches to 3 mM, and lost its PL phenomenon when 6 mM Fe(III) was added. These results demonstrate the strong interactions between the Fe(III) ions and the CDs-Mo, which indicate the CDs-Mo transfer from PEG-based fluid to the positively charged sites of steel surface and avoid undesirable friction and wear.

The Raman mapping of the worn surface lubricated with CDs-Mo was obtained, in which the change of colour represented the size of the area of D- and G-band(Fig. 3c and d). The results revealed that various elements (including carbon) deposited unevenly on the worn surface with disorder and defect structure, and the Raman spectra detected D-band and G-band in the red region with a ID/IG of 2.37, suggesting a high defect level for the deposited carbon(Fig. 3f). Moreover, some carbons derived from origin CDs-Mo were also detected. Raman spectrum in somewhere of a worn surface has a rising phenomenon and no obvious peaks, which indicates the carbon tribofilm remains high fluorescence activity (Fig. 3e, black line),[14] while somewhere out of worn surface has no Raman signal (Fig. 3e, red line).
The details of XPS spectrum of the worn surface lubricated by PEG with 1 wt% CDs-Mo was given in Supplementary information of Fig.S8 .

Two points can be summarized based on the above results, the one is that CDs-Mo have strong interactions with Fe(III), which facilitate the capture of CDs-Mo by the iron-based surface. The other is that CDs-Mo could absorb on the interface owing to both the functional groups of CDs moiety and ionic liquids moiety. With the result that the deposited CDs-Mo act as bearing balls and gradually transform to an unevenly protective oil film with rubbing time, which not only endow the excellent friction-reducing and anti-wear performances but also lead to the quench effects on PL (Fig. S9).

A nanostructured molybdenum/heteroatom-doped carbon dots nanohybrids have been prepared, which exhibit excellent tribological properties that reduced the coefficient of friction of 62.90% and wear volume of 80.21% respectively. Importantly, friction-induced luminescence quenching, transfer and aggregation of the CDs-Mo were observed and their correlations were proposed, the luminescence quenching along with the frictional duration demonstrated that the CDs-Mo transferred to interface from PEG fluid and formed carbon aggregates, thereby avoiding the direct contact of frictional pairs.
The authors declare that there is no conflict of interests regarding the publication of this article

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was financially supported by National Natural Science Foundation of China(Grant No. 21507139) and the Natural Science Foundation of Ningbo (Grant No. 2015A610051, 2016A610265).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.matlet.2019.04.107.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
